title: Slow-cooker tomato soup
subtitle: Tinned tomatoes, onion, and garlic blended smooth
excerpt: Two tins of tomatoes slow-cooked with onion, garlic, and tomato purée in vegetable stock for six hours, then blended smooth. The long cook mellows the acidity of the tomatoes without the need for cream or sugar. Finish with a swirl of cream if you like.
sourceNotes: Tomato soup is the most widely eaten soup in Britain. The tinned tomato version overtook fresh tomato soup commercially in the 1950s and has remained dominant because tinned tomatoes are more flavourful than out-of-season fresh. The slow cooker reduces the sharpness of the acidity gently over six hours without any intervention.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes well without cream for up to 3 months.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make ahead and refrigerate. Reheat gently and blend again if the texture has changed.

content.text: Use two tins of tomatoes rather than one. A single tin produces a thin, sharp soup that needs a lot of cream to balance. Two tins give body, sweetness, and a soup that tastes like tomato rather than tomato water. The slow cooker concentrates the flavour further over six hours.

Add the {{tinned-tomatoes}}, {{onion}}, {{garlic}}, {{tomato-puree}}, {{stock-vegetable}}, {{olive-oil}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}} to the slow cooker. Stir to combine. Cook on low for 6 hours. Blend smooth using a stick blender directly in the pot. Taste — if the soup is sharp, add the {{granulated-sugar}} and blend again. Swirl in {{double-cream}} to finish if using. Taste and adjust seasoning.

items.cause: Tinned tomatoes vary in sweetness by brand and season
items.fix: Add 1 teaspoon of sugar and stir in. Taste again — this usually resolves it. A pinch of bicarbonate of soda also neutralises acidity but use carefully

items.cause: Too much stock or watery tinned tomatoes
items.fix: Blend uncovered and simmer on high for 20 minutes with the lid off to reduce. Or add a spoonful of tomato purée and blend again

items.symptom: Soup tastes flat despite a long cook
items.fix: Blended soups absorb salt differently — season generously after blending. A few drops of red wine vinegar also lifts a flat tomato soup

content.text: Slow-cooker tomato soup is the most hands-off soup you can make. Everything goes in raw, the slow cooker does six hours of work, and a stick blender finishes it in two minutes. Serve with buttered white bread or grilled cheese. Freeze in individual portions for a fast weekday lunch.
